title: ophiorrhiza brunonis var. johnsonii
intro: Ophiorrhiza brunonis var. johnsonii, also known as Johnson's Woodland Milfoil, is a rare and endangered variant of the Ophiorrhiza brunonis plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Ophiorrhiza brunonis var. johnsonii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, decoctions, and powders, or used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
